content: Ketamine is an NMDA receptor antagonist with rapid antidepressant effects, often within hours, unlike traditional antidepressants taking weeks. Esketamine (S-enantiomer) is FDA-approved as nasal spray for treatment-resistant depression and depression with acute suicidal ideation. Mechanism involves glutamate surge, AMPA receptor activation, and BDNF-mediated synaptic plasticity. Dosing: esketamine 56-84mg intranasally twice weekly initially, then weekly/biweekly maintenance. Requires administration in certified clinics with 2-hour monitoring due to dissociation and blood pressure elevation risks. Response rates ~50-70% in treatment-resistant populations. Long-term safety data still emerging; potential for misuse requires careful patient selection.
